title: Corwin-Schultz high-low spread (liquidity feature, AFML Ch19.3.4)
source: LdP literature mining (2 parallel read-only agents over AFML Ch5/17/18/19 + HFT, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/extracted), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'hay alguna otra nueva feature que se puede extraer de la literatura'.
why: No feature today uses a liquidity dimension. Corwin-Schultz estimates the bid-ask spread as a fraction of price from DAILY high/low only (Corwin-Schultz 2012; AFML Snippet 19.1) — it is the microstructure measure that degrades most gracefully to daily bars (needs only high/low, which the project has; no tick/quote data). Opens a genuine liquidity dimension orthogonal to the level/momentum/vol/macro set.
value_rationale: STRONG-ADOPT (D1). Best risk/reward microstructure feature: purpose-built for daily OHLC, cheap, causal.
scope: Implement corwinSchultz (Snippet 19.1) on rolling 2-bar high/low; clamp negative alpha to 0 per the authors
Add raw spread S as feature; check empirical corr vs atr_14_norm / bb_width_20 (range/vol overlap) and prefer the volatility-netted component
If Kalman-smoothing S, use a causal filter pass only (no RTS backward smoother — lookahead)
Becker-Parkinson high-low vol (Snippet 19.2) is a near-free byproduct but overlaps rv_20 — secondary only
recommendation: Adopt on D1; cheapest path to a liquidity feature. Verify corr vs ATR before committing weight.
out_of_scope: Roll effective spread (breaks on D1: autocov often positive). Order-book/trade-size features (no data).

history.reason: LdP literature mining: Corwin-Schultz daily high-low spread = best D1 liquidity feature

corwin_schultz_spread (AFML Snippet 19.1; beta over 21-bar trailing window, authors' negative-alpha clamp) added to pipeline/microstructure.py and wired as cs_spread_21 into BOTH D1 and H4 technical builders. Scope corr check on REAL data (XAU 22y D1, EUR 27y D1, BTC H4): corr vs atr_14_norm 0.25-0.36, vs bb_width 0.13-0.30, vs rv_20 0.29-0.44 -> genuinely orthogonal, raw S adopted WITHOUT a vol-netted variant (the netting was conditional on high overlap). ~32-36% clamped zeros on real data as expected. No Kalman smoothing (out of scope); Becker-Parkinson byproduct skipped (overlaps rv_20, secondary per the entry). Registered in KNOWN_TIER2_FEATURES (B0155 registry) + both column pins updated (30->31). 5 estimator tests incl. synthetic-spread recovery, trend-dominated clamp, prefix-stability causality. Dossiers carry it on next rebuild.
